# SECTION 5: KTU 2024 Scheme Examination Question Bank & Topic Recap

> All questions below are modeled strictly on the KTU 2024 Scheme (NEP 2020) pattern: 3-mark Part A, 14-mark Part B with internal choice. Bloom's levels span **Remember, Understand, Apply, Analyze**.

---

## PART A — Short Answer Questions (3 Marks Each)

### Question 1. **[KTU University Exam – Dec 2023 – Model Paper]**
**Differentiate between SCADA and DCS in terms of functionality, architecture, and typical application scale. (3 Marks)**

**Model Answer:**

| Aspect | SCADA | DCS |
|--------|-------|-----|
| **Function** | Supervisory monitoring, data logging, alarm management | Continuous regulatory closed-loop control |
| **Architecture** | Geographically distributed, RTU-centric | Plant-centric, controller-centric |
| **Time scale** | Seconds to minutes | Milliseconds to seconds |
| **Application** | Pipelines, power grids, water networks | Refineries, chemical plants, power stations |
| **Control style** | Event-driven, setpoint changes | Continuous PID loops |

(1 mark each for the three differences – **Total 3 marks**)

---

### Question 2. **[KTU University Exam – May 2024 – Model Paper]**
**List the four main functional tiers of a SCADA system and state the role of the Historian. (3 Marks)**

**Model Answer:**
1. **Field Instrumentation Tier** — sensors & actuators.
2. **Control Tier** — PLCs / RTUs performing local control.
3. **Supervisory Tier** — MTU (SCADA server), HMI, alarms.
4. **Enterprise Tier** — MES / ERP integration.

**Role of Historian:** A time-series database that continuously archives all process tags (values, timestamps, quality codes) to enable trend analysis, regulatory reporting, root-cause analysis, and long-term optimization. *(1 mark for the role)*

---

## PART B — Long Answer Questions (14 Marks — Internal Choice)

### **Question 3A. (14 Marks)** — *SCADA Architecture, Protocols & Security*

**(a)** With a neat block diagram, describe the **four-tier architecture of a SCADA system**. Explain the role of PLCs, RTUs, and HMI in each tier. **(7 Marks)**

**(b)** Discuss the **communication protocols used in SCADA** (Modbus, OPC-UA, DNP3, IEC 60870-5-104) and explain why **OPC-UA with IEC 62443 cybersecurity** is the preferred choice in Industry 4.0 SCADA systems. **(7 Marks)**

#### Model Solution

**(a) Four-tier architecture (7 marks):**
- Tier 1 – Field: Pressure/Temperature/Flow transmitters, control valves, VFDs (4–20 mA / HART / Fieldbus). (1.5 marks)
- Tier 2 – Control: PLCs (ladder logic, fast scan) and RTUs (remote telemetry, often with built-in modem). (1.5 marks)
- Tier 3 – Supervisory: MTU (SCADA server), HMI (mimic diagrams, trends), Historian (time-series DB), Alarm engine (ISA-18.2). (2 marks)
- Tier 4 – Enterprise: MES, ERP, Cloud platforms; bidirectional data exchange via OPC-UA. (1 mark)
- A neat labelled block diagram showing data flow between tiers. (1 mark)

**(b) Protocols and security (7 marks):**
- **Modbus RTU/TCP:** simple master–slave, no security, widely used in legacy. (1.5 marks)
- **OPC-UA:** modern, object-oriented, semantic interoperability, supports authentication & encryption. (1.5 marks)
- **DNP3 / IEC 60870-5-104:** substation automation and electric utility SCADA. (1 mark)
- **OPC-UA + IEC 62443:** provides information security zoning, secure authentication, role-based access, and TLS 1.3 encryption — the de-facto Industry 4.0 standard. (2 marks)
- Comparison table of features. (1 mark)

---

### **Question 3B. (14 Marks)** — *Industry 4.0, CPS & AI in Manufacturing*

**(a)** Define **Industry 4.0** and explain the **nine pillars of Industry 4.0** with one-line descriptions each. **(7 Marks)**

**(b)** With a suitable diagram, explain the **integration of Cyber-Physical Systems (CPS) and AI into the SCADA layer** of a process plant. Discuss at least **three AI use-cases** (predictive maintenance, soft sensing, energy optimization) with their engineering impact. **(7 Marks)**

#### Model Solution

**(a) Industry 4.0 definition & pillars (7 marks):**
- **Definition:** Industry 4.0 = fourth industrial revolution; integration of CPS, IIoT, AI, cloud, big-data into manufacturing for smart, self-optimizing factories. (1 mark)
- **Nine pillars** (0.5 mark each = 4.5 marks, plus a brief intro 1.5 marks for context):
  1. Cyber-Physical Systems (CPS)
  2. Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT)
  3. Cloud & Edge Computing
  4. Big Data & Analytics
  5. Artificial Intelligence (AI) / ML
  6. Augmented & Virtual Reality (AR / VR)
  7. Digital Twin
  8. Additive Manufacturing
  9. Autonomous Robots & Cobots

**(b) CPS + AI + SCADA integration (7 marks):**
- Labelled diagram showing Field → PLC/RTU → SCADA → AI Engine → Operator. (2 marks)
- **Predictive Maintenance:** ML models (XGBoost / LSTM) trained on vibration/temperature data predict RUL → reduce unplanned downtime by 30–50 %. (1.5 marks)
- **Soft Sensing:** AI estimates unmeasurable variables (e.g., polymer viscosity) from secondary measurements → eliminates expensive hardware analyzers. (1.5 marks)
- **Energy Optimization:** Reinforcement-learning agents tune setpoints to minimize kWh consumption while maintaining quality → 15–25 % energy savings. (1.5 marks)
- Concluding remark on Industry 4.0 transformation. (0.5 marks)

> [!WARNING]
> **KTU Examiner's Valuation Warning — Common Pitfalls:**
> 1. **Confusing SCADA with DCS:** SCADA is *supervisory* (Level 2); DCS is *regulatory* (Level 1). Marks deducted if interchanged.
> 2. **Treating AI as "magic":** AI works on *data*; you must mention the data pipeline (acquisition → preprocessing → model → inference → action).
> 3. **Skipping the protocol layer:** Always mention **OPC-UA** explicitly in modern SCADA answers.
> 4. **Forgetting units & numerical verification** in linearization problems (e.g., Section 3.1 type).
> 5. **Not labelling the ISA-95 levels** when drawing diagrams — at least 1 mark lost.
> 6. **Writing generic definitions** without tying back to *process* industries (refinery, power, water, chemicals) — examiners expect domain examples.

---

## 📌 Topic Recap & Important Things to Remember

> **Quick-look revision bullets (high-yield for the last-night read):**

- ⏱ **SCADA = Supervisory (Level 2)**, **DCS = Regulatory (Level 1)**. Do not confuse in exams.
- ⏱ **Four SCADA tiers:** Field → Control (PLC/RTU) → Supervisory (MTU/HMI/Historian) → Enterprise (MES/ERP/Cloud).
- ⏱ **ISA-95 / IEC 62264** five-level pyramid: ERP → MES → SCADA → DCS/PLC → Field.
- ⏱ **4–20 mA linearization:** $E = 12.5\,m - 50$ (for 0–200 kPa); live-zero at 4 mA = cable-break detection.
- ⏱ **Key SCADA protocols:** Modbus (legacy), **OPC-UA (Industry 4.0 standard)**, DNP3, IEC 60870-5-104.
- ⏱ **Cybersecurity:** IEC 62443 + TLS 1.3 in modern SCADA.
- ⏱ **SCADA scan rate:** $R_{\text{scan}} = T_{\text{cycle}}/N_{\text{tags}}$; latency = acquisition + comm + processing + historian write.
- ⏱ **Availability:** $A = \text{MTBF} / (\text{MTBF} + \text{MTTR})$; "five nines" = 99.999 %.
- ⏱ **Industry 4.0 =** CPS + IIoT + Cloud + Big Data + AI + AR/VR + Digital Twin + Additive Mfg + Cobots.
- ⏱ **Smart Manufacturing** = Industry 4.0 *operationalized* inside a factory.
- ⏱ **CPS = Computation + Communication + Control**, deeply integrated with a physical process.
- ⏱ **CPS state equations:** $\dot{x}_p = f_p(x_p, u, w)$ and $\dot{x}_c = f_c(x_c, y, r)$.
- ⏱ **AI in manufacturing:** predictive maintenance, soft sensing, energy optimization, quality prediction, RL-based control.
- ⏱ **Digital Twin** = real-time virtual replica of the physical process, used for what-if simulation and AI training.
- ⏱ **OEE = Availability × Performance × Quality** — KPI to evaluate smart-factory effectiveness.
- ⏱ **Exam mantra:** Always draw a **labelled block diagram** (with ISA-95 levels) — easy 2–3 marks.
